This gives realistic measurement scatter — occasional outliers included — rather than the flat uniform distribution that a plain NextDouble() would produce.

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text:
• No CV/CC transition for the power supply — the output is always in CV mode in simulation.
  • No fault conditions — no OVP/OCP trip, no instrument error states.
  • No waveform math — oscilloscope MATH channel queries return generic values.
  • No multi-instrument coupling — e.g., the power supply's simulated output voltage does not affect a simulated DMM connected in parallel.
  • Single-instance state — each SimulatedConnectionProvider holds its own state. Two simulated connections to the same SIM::GPIB0::22::INSTR resource string are independent.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Agilent 34970A is a modular data acquisition and switching unit that accepts plug-in cards for analog measurement, digital I/O, and DAC output.

Specification Value Type DAQ / Switch Unit Interface GPIB, USB-TMC Cards supported 34901A (20-ch analog mux), 34907A (DIO + DAC) Driver name Agilent34970A Resource patterns GPIB?*::?*::INSTR, USB?*::?*::INSTR

docs.title: 34901A — 20-Channel Multiplexer (Analog Input)
docs.text:
• 20 channels: (@101) … (@120)
  • Measures: DC voltage, AC voltage, 2W resistance, 4W resistance, DC current, AC current, temperature (thermocouple / RTD)
  • Two-wire and four-wire measurement modes
  • Maximum 250 V (channel-to-earth)

docs.title: 34907A — Digital I/O + DAC Module
docs.text:
• Two 8-bit digital I/O ports (A and B)
  • Two DAC channels with 16-bit resolution, 0–12 V range
  • Digital channels: (@201) … (@216)
  • DAC channels: (@221), (@222)

docs.title: Connecting
docs.text:
1. Power on the 34970A with cards installed
  2. Click Add Instrument
  3. Select the GPIB or USB resource
  4. Auto-detection reads *IDN? and matches the driver
  5. Click Connect

The driver queries the installed cards and populates the front panel with the detected configuration.

docs.title: Front Panel
docs.text: Control Description Card Slot View Shows installed cards and available channels Scan Triggers a single scan across all configured channels Channel Readings Grid of latest readings per channel DAC Output (34907A) Voltage set-point for DAC1 and DAC2 Digital I/O (34907A) Byte-level read/write for Port A and Port B

docs.title: Agilent34970A_ScanChannels
docs.text:
Scans a list of channels and stores results.

Property Type Description Instrument InstrumentSelector Connected 34970A instance Channels Text Channel list, e.g. (@101:110,201) Function Combo VOLT:DC, VOLT:AC, RES, FRES, CURR:DC, CURR:AC, TEMP Variable Prefix Text Variables {prefix}_101, {prefix}_102, … receive channel readings

docs.title: Agilent34970A_MeasureVoltage
docs.text:
Measures DC voltage on a single channel.

Property Type Description Instrument InstrumentSelector Connected 34970A instance Channel Number Channel number, e.g. 101 Range Number 0 = Auto Variable Variable Receives the measured voltage in V

docs.title: Agilent34970A_MeasureTemperature
docs.text:
Measures temperature via thermocouple or RTD.

Property Type Description Instrument InstrumentSelector Connected 34970A instance Channel Number Channel number Transducer Combo TC (thermocouple) or RTD TC Type Combo K, J, T, E, R, S, B, N (for thermocouple) Variable Variable Receives temperature in °C

docs.title: Agilent34970A_SetDAC
docs.text:
Sets a DAC output voltage (requires 34907A card).

Property Type Description Instrument InstrumentSelector Connected 34970A instance DAC Channel Combo DAC1 (@221) or DAC2 (@222) Voltage Number Output voltage, 0 … 12 V

docs.title: Agilent34970A_SetDigitalOutput
docs.text:
Sets an 8-bit value on a DIO port (requires 34907A card).

Property Type Description Instrument InstrumentSelector Connected 34970A instance Port Combo Port A or Port B Value Number Byte value 0–255

docs.title: Example Sequence — Multi-Channel Temperature Scan
docs.text:
[Start]
→ [Loop N=10, counter=i]
    ↓ body
    [Agilent34970A_ScanChannels, Channels=(@101:104), Function=TEMP, TC=K, Prefix=ch]
    → [AddToChart, Series="CH101 Temp", Variable=ch_101]
    → [Wait 5000ms]
→ [SaveCsv, Path="C:\Data\temp_scan.csv"]
→ [End]

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The CTS T-40/50 is an environmental test chamber for temperature and humidity testing. It uses a proprietary binary ASCII protocol over RS-232 — not VISA/SCPI.

Specification Value Type Environmental Test Chamber Temperature range −75 … +185 °C Interface RS-232 (COM port), 19200 baud, 8N1 with odd parity Protocol Custom binary ASCII framing (STX / ADR / DATA / CHK / ETX) Driver name CTSChamber Resource patterns COM?*

docs.title: Serial Connection Settings
docs.text:
The CTS chamber does not use VISA. The driver uses a custom IConnectionProvider that opens a COM port directly:

Parameter Value Baud rate 19200 Data bits 8 Parity Odd Stop bits 1 Flow control None

docs.title: Connecting
docs.text:
1. Connect the CTS chamber's RS-232 port to the PC (use a USB-to-RS232 adapter if needed)
  2. Note the COM port number from Device Manager
  3. Click Add Instrument and select the COM port resource (e.g. COM3)
  4. The Connection Manager lists COM ports automatically
  5. Select the CTSChamber driver from the drop-down (auto-detection is not possible because the chamber does not respond to *IDN?)
  6. Click Connect

USB-to-RS232 adapters

Use a high-quality adapter with a genuine FTDI or Prolific chip. Cheap adapters often lose data at 19200 baud with odd parity.

docs.title: Protocol Details
docs.text:
The CTS chamber uses a proprietary binary ASCII framing scheme. Developers who need to extend the driver should read the CTSSerialConnectionProvider documentation.

Frame structure:

STX  |  ADR  |  DATA (each byte OR'd with 0x80)  |  CHK  |  ETX
0x02    0x30    0xC2 0xD4 ...                        0xHH   0x03

The checksum (CHK) is the XOR of all bytes from ADR to the last DATA byte.

docs.text: Control Description Set Temperature (°C) Target temperature Set Ramp (°C/min) Temperature ramp rate Start Start the chamber program Stop Stop the chamber Pause Pause the current program Temperature readback Current chamber temperature in °C Status Chamber operating mode (Running, Paused, Idle, Error)

docs.text:

Property Type Description Instrument InstrumentSelector Connected CTS chamber instance

docs.text:

Property Type Description Instrument InstrumentSelector Connected CTS chamber instance

docs.text:

Property Type Description Instrument InstrumentSelector Connected CTS chamber instance Variable Variable Receives temperature in °C

docs.text:
Blocks execution until the chamber reaches a temperature within a tolerance band, or a timeout expires.

Property Type Description Instrument InstrumentSelector Connected CTS chamber instance Target (°C) Number Target temperature Tolerance (°C) Number Acceptable deviation band (e.g. 0.5 means ±0.5 °C) Timeout (s) Number Maximum wait time in seconds. 0 = no timeout.

docs.title: Example Sequence — Temperature Profile Test
docs.text:
[Start]
→ [CTSChamber_SetRamp, Rate=2.0]
→ [CTSChamber_Start]
→ [Loop temperatures in -40, 25, 85°C: use SetVariable]
    ↓
    [SetVariable, Name=targetTemp, Value=-40]
    → [CTSChamber_SetTemperature, Temp={targetTemp}]
    → [CTSChamber_WaitForTemperature, Target={targetTemp}, Tolerance=0.5, Timeout=3600]
    → [Wait 300000ms]   # soak 5 minutes
    → [HP34401A_MeasureDCV, Variable=voltage]
    → [SaveCsv, Path="C:\Data\temp_profile.csv", Append=true]
→ [CTSChamber_Stop]
→ [End]

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The HP 34401A (also marketed as Agilent 34401A and Keysight 34401A) is a classic 6½-digit bench multimeter capable of DC/AC voltage and current, resistance, frequency, period, diode, and continuity measurements.

Specification Value Type 6½-digit DMM Interface GPIB, USB-TMC, RS-232 SCPI command set Yes Driver name HP34401A Resource patterns GPIB?*::?*::INSTR, USB?*::?*::INSTR, ASRL?*::INSTR

docs.title: Connecting
docs.text:
1. Connect the instrument via GPIB, USB, or RS-232
  2. Click Add Instrument in the sidebar
  3. The Connection Manager scans and lists the resource (e.g. GPIB0::1::INSTR)
  4. Select the resource — the *IDN? response is parsed and the HP34401A driver is highlighted automatically
  5. Click Connect

docs.title: Front Panel
docs.text:
The HP34401A front panel provides:

Control Description DCV button Measure DC voltage ACV button Measure AC voltage (true RMS) DCI button Measure DC current ACI button Measure AC current Ω 2W button Measure resistance (2-wire) Ω 4W button Measure resistance (4-wire, Kelvin) Freq button Measure frequency Period button Measure period Diode button Diode forward voltage Cont button Continuity (beeps if below threshold) Range drop-down Auto or fixed range Reading display Shows last measured value Continuous toggle Auto-repeat measurements every second

docs.title: Sequence Blocks
docs.text:
All HP34401A blocks share a common set of properties:

Property Type Description Instrument InstrumentSelector The connected HP34401A instance to use Range Number Measurement range (0 = Auto) Variable Name Variable Variable that receives the measured value

docs.title: HP34401A_MeasureDCV
docs.text:
Sends CONF:VOLT:DC {range},DEF then READ?.

Returns voltage in V.

docs.title: HP34401A_MeasureACV
docs.text:
Sends CONF:VOLT:AC {range},DEF then READ?.

Returns RMS AC voltage in V.

docs.title: HP34401A_MeasureDCI
docs.text:
Sends CONF:CURR:DC {range},DEF then READ?.

Returns current in A.

docs.title: HP34401A_MeasureACI
docs.text:
Sends CONF:CURR:AC {range},DEF then READ?.

Returns RMS AC current in A.

docs.title: HP34401A_MeasureResistance2W
docs.text:
Sends CONF:RES {range},DEF then READ?.

Returns resistance in Ω.

docs.title: HP34401A_MeasureResistance4W
docs.text:
Sends CONF:FRES {range},DEF then READ?.

Returns resistance in Ω. Use 4-wire for low-resistance measurements to eliminate lead resistance.

docs.title: HP34401A_MeasureFrequency
docs.text:
Sends CONF:FREQ {range},DEF then READ?.

Returns frequency in Hz.

docs.title: HP34401A_MeasurePeriod
docs.text:
Sends CONF:PER {range},DEF then READ?.

Returns period in s.

docs.title: Example Sequence — DC Voltage Log
docs.text:
[Start]
→ [Loop N=60, counter=i]
    ↓ body
    [HP34401A_MeasureDCV, Range=10, Variable=voltage]
    → [AddToChart, Series="Battery Voltage", Variable=voltage]
    → [Wait 1000ms]
→ [SaveCsv, Path="C:\Data\battery_test.csv"]
→ [End]

This sequence measures DC voltage once per second for 60 seconds and saves the results to a CSV file.

docs.title: SCPI Command Reference
docs.text:
The driver uses the following SCPI commands:

Action Command Configure DCV CONF:VOLT:DC {range},DEF Configure ACV CONF:VOLT:AC {range},DEF Configure DCI CONF:CURR:DC {range},DEF Configure ACI CONF:CURR:AC {range},DEF Configure Resistance 2W CONF:RES {range},DEF Configure Resistance 4W CONF:FRES {range},DEF Configure Frequency CONF:FREQ {range},DEF Configure Period CONF:PER {range},DEF Trigger and read READ? Identification *IDN? Reset *RST

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The ITECH IT6922B is a 60 V / 5 A programmable DC power supply with SCPI control over USB, LAN, or GPIB.

Specification Value Type DC Power Supply Voltage range 0 … 60 V Current range 0 … 5 A Interface USB-TMC, LAN (VXI-11), GPIB Driver name ItechIT6922B Resource patterns USB?*::?*::INSTR, TCPIP?*::?*::INSTR, GPIB?*::?*::INSTR

docs.title: Connecting
docs.text:
1. Connect via USB, LAN, or GPIB
  2. Click Add Instrument and select the resource
  3. Auto-detection matches the *IDN? response (ITECH ELECTRONIC,IT6922B)
  4. Click Connect

Output safety

Always set the voltage and current limit before enabling the output. The power supply will immediately apply the set voltage when the output is enabled.

Sends: MEAS:VOLT?

docs.text:

docs.title: Example Sequence — Voltage Sweep
docs.text:
[Start]
→ [ItechIT6922B_SetCurrent, I=0.5A]
→ [ItechIT6922B_SetOutput, Output=ON]
→ [Loop N=6, counter=i]
    ↓ body
    [MathBlock, Expression={i}*2+1, Result=setV]     # 1,3,5,7,9,11 V
    → [ItechIT6922B_SetVoltage, Voltage={setV}]
    → [Wait 500ms]
    → [ItechIT6922B_MeasureVoltage, Variable=vActual]
    → [ItechIT6922B_MeasureCurrent, Variable=iActual]
    → [AddToChart, Series="Voltage", Variable=vActual]
    → [SaveCsv, Path="C:\Data\sweep.csv", Append=true]
→ [ItechIT6922B_SetOutput, Output=OFF]
→ [End]

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Keithley 2000 is a high-accuracy 6½-digit multimeter with a SCPI command set similar to the HP 34401A.

Specification Value Type 6½-digit DMM Interface GPIB, USB-TMC Driver name Keithley2000 Resource patterns GPIB?*::?*::INSTR, USB?*::?*::INSTR

docs.title: Connecting
docs.text:
1. Connect the Keithley 2000 via GPIB or USB
  2. Click Add Instrument and select the resource
  3. Auto-detection parses the *IDN? response (KEITHLEY INSTRUMENTS INC.,MODEL 2000)
  4. Click Connect

docs.title: Front Panel
docs.text: The Keithley 2000 front panel is identical in layout to the HP 34401A front panel — it provides the same set of measurement buttons, range selector, and continuous mode toggle.

docs.title: Sequence Blocks
docs.text:
The Keithley 2000 provides the same eight measurement blocks as the HP 34401A. All blocks share the same properties: Instrument, Range, Variable Name.

Block Measurement Unit Keithley2000_MeasureDCV DC Voltage V Keithley2000_MeasureACV AC Voltage (RMS) V Keithley2000_MeasureDCI DC Current A Keithley2000_MeasureACI AC Current (RMS) A Keithley2000_MeasureResistance2W 2-wire Resistance Ω Keithley2000_MeasureResistance4W 4-wire Resistance Ω Keithley2000_MeasureFrequency Frequency Hz Keithley2000_MeasurePeriod Period s

docs.title: SCPI Commands
docs.text:
The driver sends standard IEEE 488.2 / SCPI commands compatible with the Keithley 2000 command reference:

Action Command Configure DCV CONF:VOLT:DC {range},DEF Configure ACV CONF:VOLT:AC {range},DEF Trigger + read READ? Identification *IDN? Reset *RST

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Rigol DS1054Z and DS1104Z are 4-channel digital oscilloscopes with 50 MHz and 100 MHz bandwidth respectively. They connect via LAN (TCPIP), USB, or GPIB and communicate over standard VISA.

Specification Value Type Digital Oscilloscope Models DS1054Z (50 MHz), DS1104Z (100 MHz) Channels 4 analog Interface LAN (TCPIP), USB-TMC, GPIB Driver name RigolDS1000Z Resource patterns TCPIP?*::?*::INSTR, TCPIP?*::hislip?*::INSTR, USB?*::0x1AB1::?*::INSTR, GPIB?*::?*::INSTR

docs.title: Connecting
docs.text:
1. Connect the DS1000Z via LAN or USB
  2. Click Add Instrument and select the resource
  3. Auto-detection parses *IDN? (RIGOL TECHNOLOGIES,DS1054Z or DS1104Z)
  4. Click Connect

For LAN: find the instrument IP address in its Utility → IO Setting → LAN menu, then enter the resource manually in the Connection Manager: TCPIP0::192.168.x.x::inst0::INSTR

docs.title: Example Sequence — Single-Shot Capture with Measurement
docs.text:
[Start]
→ [RigolDS1000Z_SetChannel, Channel=1, Coupling=DC, V/div=1, Probe×=10]
→ [RigolDS1000Z_SetTimebase, s/div=0.001, Mode=MAIN]
→ [RigolDS1000Z_SetTrigger, Mode=EDGE, Sweep=SINGle, Source=CH1, Slope=POSitive, Level=0.5]
→ [RigolDS1000Z_Single, WaitMs=2000]
→ [RigolDS1000Z_MeasureVoltage, Channel=1, Parameter=VPP, OutputVariable=vpp]
→ [RigolDS1000Z_MeasureTime, Channel=1, Parameter=FREQuency, OutputVariable=freq]
→ [LogMessage "VPP={vpp} V  Freq={freq} Hz"]
→ [RigolDS1000Z_ReadWaveform, Channel=1, WaveMode=NORMal, FilePath=C:\Data\waveform.csv]
→ [SaveCsv, Path=C:\Data\results.csv]
→ [End]

docs.title: Example Sequence — FFT Analysis
docs.text:
[Start]
→ [RigolDS1000Z_SetChannel, Channel=1, Coupling=DC, V/div=0.5, Probe×=1]
→ [RigolDS1000Z_SetTimebase, s/div=0.0001]
→ [RigolDS1000Z_Run]
→ [WaitBlock, 500]
→ [RigolDS1000Z_MathSetup, Operation=FFT, Source1=CH1, FFTWindow=HANNing, FFTUnit=DB, Enable=true]
→ [End]
